annotations.label: Tuple

anchor.snippet: a tuple is a finite sequence (or ordered list) of numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib models tuples as functions `Fin n → α` (or dependent versions) with the standard `Fin.cons`/`Fin.snoc` API.

annotations.label: n-tuple

anchor.snippet: An n -tuple is a tuple of n elements

annotations.note: An n-tuple in Mathlib is just a function `Fin n → α`, indexed via the finite type `Fin n`.

annotations.label: Empty tuple

anchor.snippet: There is only one 0-tuple, called the empty tuple

annotations.note: The unique function `Fin 0 → α` is given by `Fin.elim0`, witnessing the empty tuple.

annotations.label: Singleton and ordered pair

anchor.snippet: A 1-tuple and a 2-tuple are commonly called a singleton and an ordered pair

annotations.note: Ordered pairs are formalized as the core `Prod` type, and 1-tuples are `Fin.cons x Fin.elim0`.

annotations.label: Formal definition of n-tuple as function

anchor.snippet: An n -tuple can be formally defined as the image of a function that has the set of the first n natural numbers as its domain

annotations.note: Mathlib's tuples are by definition functions `Fin n → α`, exactly the function-with-domain-{0,…,n−1} model.

annotations.label: Recursive definition from ordered pairs

anchor.snippet: Tuples may be also defined from ordered pairs by a recurrence starting from an ordered pair

annotations.note: Mathlib uses the `Fin n → α` model, not a nested-pair recursive construction.

annotations.label: Null/empty tuple naming

anchor.snippet: The unique 0-tuple is called the null tuple or empty tuple

annotations.note: This is a naming convention, not a formal statement.

annotations.label: Complex number as 2-tuple

anchor.snippet: a complex number can be represented as a 2‑tuple of reals

annotations.note: `Complex.equivRealProd` gives the equivalence `ℂ ≃ ℝ × ℝ`.

annotations.label: Identity of tuples

anchor.snippet: The general rule for the identity of two n -tuples is

annotations.note: Two functions (hence tuples `Fin n → α`) are equal iff their components agree, via `funext_iff`.

annotations.label: Tuple distinguished from set

anchor.snippet: Thus a tuple has properties that distinguish it from a set

annotations.note: Meta-comparison between tuple and set semantics, not a formal theorem.

annotations.label: Tuple as empty function

anchor.snippet: tuple may be identified as the empty function

annotations.note: `Fin.elim0` is the unique function out of the empty type `Fin 0`, i.e. the empty tuple.

annotations.label: Tuple as surjective function

anchor.snippet: may be identified with the surjective function

annotations.note: Mathlib does not identify tuples specifically with the surjective function onto their image.

annotations.label: Function as graph

anchor.snippet: Functions are commonly identified with their graphs

annotations.note: Mathlib treats functions as primitive; there is no canonical `Function.graph` identifying a function with its set-theoretic graph.

annotations.label: Tuples as nested ordered pairs

anchor.snippet: Another way of modeling tuples in set theory is as nested ordered pairs

annotations.note: Mathlib does not use a nested-ordered-pair model for tuples.

annotations.label: 0-tuple as empty set

anchor.snippet: The 0-tuple (i.e. the empty tuple) is represented by the empty set

annotations.note: Mathlib does not encode tuples as set-theoretic objects this way.

annotations.label: n-tuple as nested ordered pair

anchor.snippet: An n -tuple, with n > 0 , can be defined as an ordered pair of its first entry and an

annotations.note: The recursive (a, (n−1)-tuple) encoding is not Mathlib's model.

annotations.label: Variant peeling from the other end

anchor.snippet: A variant of this definition starts "peeling off" elements from the other end

annotations.note: This set-theoretic variant is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tuples as nested sets (Kuratowski)

anchor.snippet: Using Kuratowski's representation for an ordered pair , the second definition above can be reformulated in terms of pure set theory

annotations.note: `ZFSet.pair` defines the Kuratowski ordered pair in ZFC, but Mathlib does not iterate it to form general n-tuples.

annotations.label: Number of n-tuples of an m-set

anchor.snippet: The number of n -tuples of an m -set is m n

annotations.note: `Fintype.card_fun` states `card (α → β) = card β ^ card α`, which gives `m^n` for `α = Fin n`.

annotations.label: Tuple as product type

anchor.snippet: a tuple has a product type

annotations.note: Product types are realized by `Prod` (and iterated products) in core Lean/Mathlib.

annotations.label: Record type for labeled tuple

anchor.snippet: The tuple with labeled elements used in the relational model has a record type

annotations.note: Lean's `structure` mechanism implements record types but there is no Mathlib decl modeling labeled relational tuples.

annotations.label: Unit type interpretation

anchor.snippet: The unit type has as semantic interpretation the 0-tuple

annotations.note: Lean's `PUnit`/`Unit` is the unique-inhabitant unit type, serving as the 0-tuple.
